Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Mila Popov For Her Failures To Comply With Her Responsibilities In Discovery And False Statements Made During The Discovery Process
Off Calendar Nov-14-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KELLY M. MANNION, Esq. of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler, P.C. (Tel: 415-733-1050) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CSAA FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Mila Popov For Her Failures To Comply With Her Responsibilities In Discovery And False Statements Made During The Discovery Process.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion for terminating sanctions is DENIED. Defendants have not established sufficient "exceptional" circumstances justifying terminating sanctions in the absence of a prior motion compelling discovery. It is true that the record reflects some points of obfuscation by Plaintiff and her lawyers, but it does not rise to "exceptional" circumstances. The motion for evidentiary sanctions is DENIED on similar grounds, however, it may be renewed before the trial judge as a motion in limine.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jun-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. J. EDWARD KERLEY, Esq. of Kerley Schaffer LLP (Tel: 510-379-5801) for movant/counsel of record for plaintiff Mila Popov;
Atty. JANESSA CHUN, Esq. of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth (Tel: 650-592-5400) for defendant CSAA Fire and Casualty Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD RE: PLAINTIFF MILA POPOV.

Matter called. Defense counsel does not object to the motion. The Court passes the matter to conduct an unreported in camera conference call at 10:00 a.m. with Mr. Kerley.

The Court conducts an unreported in camera conference call with Mr. Kerley. The Court rules as follows: Attorney Mr. Kerley's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Mila Popov is granted. Order signed.
